messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.

#if defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)

#    pragma once
#    include <array>
#    include <iostream>
#    include <string>
#    include <string_view>

///TODO: to be used Later to refactor and in decoupling
auto dummy = false;

#else
#    error OSC_IPC.cpp only compiled when defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)
#endif (why says red line on line 13 error keyword?)

The issue in your code is that you cannot have any arguments with the `#endif` preprocessor directive. The `#endif` directive should not have any condition or argument. It should just close the corresponding `#if`, `#ifdef`, or `#ifndef` directive.

Here is the corrected version of your code:

```cpp
#if defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)

#    pragma once
#    include <array>
#    include <iostream>
#    include <string>
#    include <string_view>

///TODO: to be used Later to refactor and in decoupling
auto dummy = false;

#else
#    error OSC_IPC.cpp only compiled when defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)
#endif
```

The red line shown on line 13 is because of the incorrect syntax of the `#endif` directive. After correcting it, the error should disappear.

i still dont know here is the code #if defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)

#    pragma once
#    include <array>
#    include <iostream>
#    include <string>
#    include <string_view>

///TODO: to be used Later to refactor and in decoupling
auto dummy = false;

#else
#    error OSC_IPC.cpp only compiled when defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)
#endif

I see the confusion. The `#else` directive should not have any condition. It should be used to provide an alternative code block when the condition in the `#if` directive is not met. If you want to check for a condition in the `#else` block, you can use `#ifndef`.

Here is the corrected version of your code:

```cpp
#if defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)

#    pragma once
#    include <array>
#    include <iostream>
#    include <string>
#    include <string_view>

///TODO: to be used Later to refactor and in decoupling
auto dummy = false;

#else
#    error OSC_IPC.cpp only compiled when defined(ATT_OS_WINDOWS) && defined(FORCE_OSC)
#endif
```

This code snippet will now properly handle the conditional compilation for the defined macros.
